page.introText: Discover your dominant GEM personality style through a practical assessment built around everyday situations.
page.quizTip: Tip: Don't pick what sounds ideal. Choose the first answer that feels true. What would you actually do, not what you think you should do?

seo.metaDescription: Take the GEM Personality Assessment to discover whether your dominant style is Sapphire, Ruby, Pearl, or Emerald.
seo.keywords: GEM personality assessment, personality test, Sapphire Ruby Pearl Emerald
seo.ogDescription: Discover your GEM personality style with a practical assessment from Thynkwise.

questions.question: SITUATION 1: You have received an item that needs to be assembled.

answers.answerText: Throw instructions, only look at pictures.
Grumble, assemble fast, toss leftovers.
Do it alone, take more time.
Read all instructions and take inventory.
Inside joke or gag gift.
Buy something great, keep it, buy another.
Make it yourself.
Buy practical gift, possibly a card.

questions.question: SITUATION 3: Hotel chains ask guests to reuse towels to save water. Your response is:

answers.answerText: You probably didn’t notice the card and throw the towel on the floor, in a hurry.
You expect full service for the price paid and prefer fresh towels daily.
You follow instructions and hang towels to contribute to environmental conservation.
You analyze hotel sincerity but hang towels anyway because leaving them on the floor seems inappropriate.

questions.question: SITUATION 4: Ordering from a menu at a restaurant with others:

answers.answerText: You wait to order something different to taste multiple unique items.
You gravitate toward the most expensive item, seeking the best quality.
You choose the least expensive and healthier option, especially if someone else might pay.
You consider cost to value, making the most sensible choice.

questions.question: SITUATION 5: Server delivers wrong dish during a busy dinner:

answers.answerText: Eat it anyway; might be better. Mention casually without expectation.
Immediately point out the mistake, questioning server's competence.
Say nothing to avoid stressing the server further.
Politely request correction and possibly a discount.
Rules are meant to be broken.
Your rules should be followed; others' rules are stupid.
You follow rules to avoid displeasing others.
Rules are meant to be strictly followed.
Working directly with people, relationships, and travel.
Challenging roles that offer the most money.
Helping, serving people, working toward a great cause.
Working with methods, systems, numbers, and routines.
You love team sports and value participation over winning.
Winning is essential; losing is not an option.
Participation is fine, but you prefer not to compete.
Sports seem pointless compared to tasks and research you prefer.
Bright, trendy, fun clothing, even humorous.
High-end labels and expensive clothes; dress to impress.
Comfortable, breathable clothing; family heirlooms and earth tones.
Timeless clothing kept for long-term use due to practicality.
Colorful, distinctive shoes; comfort isn't priority.
Top-of-the-line, brand-name shoes, several pairs in black.
Comfortable, easy slip-on shoes, practical for daily wear.
Quality shoes bought on discount, worn until completely worn out.
Lots of inexpensive or fake accessories; possibly tattoos or piercings.
High-quality, expensive jewelry intended to impress.
Minimal, handmade or sentimental jewelry.
Simple and tasteful accessories, possibly just a watch or wedding band.
Often got into trouble, loud and tardy.
Best at chosen important activities.
Quiet, compliant, diligent student.
Never in trouble, neat, organized, scored high in math/science.

questions.question: SITUATION 13: When it comes to your car:

answers.answerText: A fun car with cool colors, expensive sound system, messy interior.
Clean, sparkling, attention-grabbing high-end brand.
Safe, environmentally friendly, equipped for family needs.
Cost-effective, reliable, well-maintained and organized.
Fast, distracted driving with loud music, ignoring pedestrians.
Aggressive, honking, tailgating, ignoring bike lanes.
Cautious, considerate, stopping for pedestrians and animals.
Strictly obeying traffic laws, careful adherence to speed limits.

questions.question: SITUATION 15: When packing to go out of town:

answers.answerText: Unorganized, clothes and toiletries mixed together haphazardly.
Packing last minute, extra clothes, focused on appearance.
Minimalistic, considerate of others, prepared for emergencies.
Carefully organized, outfits planned, toiletries neatly separated.

questions.question: SITUATION 16: When it comes to dating:

answers.answerText: Active, fun outings, likely late due to indecision on attire.
Prefer expensive restaurants.
Quiet, modest places with organic options.
Punctual, appropriately dressed for the occasion.
Actively engaging and playing for hours.
Multitasking, distracted with work and communication.
Encouraging quiet, calm activities.
Finds kids noisy, difficult to control.

questions.question: SITUATION 18: When it comes to money:

answers.answerText: Impulsive, uncertain where money goes.
Splurging on expensive items.
Generous to friends and family, cautious spender.
Meticulous budgeting, no impulse buys.
Fun-oriented, enthusiastic starter, rarely completes.
Ambitious, sets lofty goals, avoids failure.
Supportive, volunteers to help others achieve their goals.
Practical, assesses and sets achievable goals.
Talks out problems verbally with others.
Quick, decisive, confident in solutions.
Feels overwhelmed, seeks help to avoid disappointment.
Methodically maps out and selects the most efficient solution.

questions.question: SITUATION 21: An argument breaks out, you are the one who:

answers.answerText: Try to make light of it and attempt to change the subject.
Probably started it (but you would never admit that).
Will quiet down and stay out of it.
Have a valid argument and lay it out, even if it’s harsh.

questions.question: SITUATION 22: If it’s a big decision:

answers.answerText: Make quick decisions and often change them just as quickly.
Make quick decisions, not afraid of tough choices, disregarding obstacles.
Never like making decisions, quickly become stressed, worried about hurting feelings.
Consider all options and make the most logical choice.

questions.question: SITUATION 23: If there was a threat of a natural disaster in your area:

answers.answerText: Run to the store and stock up on snacks and games at the last minute.
Too busy to think about it until it happens, then quickly buy supplies.
Prepared with excess supplies for yourself and others.
Always have a fully stocked emergency kit ready in advance.
Everything is everywhere, disorganized but still able to find things.
Quick clean-up, discarding things carelessly to fit a car inside.
Struggles to discard old items, donates to those in need.
Organized perfectly with a color-coded labeling system.
Mostly empty hangers, clothes scattered on the floor.
Not very organized, but clothes are hanging due to busy schedule.
Packed with hand-me-downs and comfortable, organic clothes.
Highly organized, clothes sorted from light to dark, everything neat.
Load dirty dishes carelessly, often damaging items.
Half-loaded dishwasher frequently run.
Waits until dishwasher is fully loaded to conserve resources.
Carefully pre-rinsed, orderly loaded dishes.
Spend too long, buying extras, forgetting initial items.
Quick grab-and-go shopping.
Immediately head to organic section.
Uses list, buys only what’s necessary, seeks discounts.
Outgoing, constantly making and maintaining numerous friends.
Ambitious friends, ensures everyone knows you.
Kind-hearted, maintains deep long-term friendships.
Few close friends, cautious with trust.

questions.question: SITUATION 29: In a room full of people:

answers.answerText: Talk comfortably to everyone in the room.
Identify competition immediately.
Engage deeply with one person.
Sit back and observe from a distance.

questions.question: SITUATION 30: When talking on the phone:

answers.answerText: Extended casual conversations, even with strangers.
Purpose-driven conversations ending with clear actions.
Mostly listening, available for as long as needed.
Concise, questions upfront, no lingering.
Very animated, enthusiastic, minimal notes, sometimes disorganized.
Confident, comfortable presenting first, direct speaker.
Avoids public speaking, keeps speeches short and soft-spoken.
Step-by-step organized presentation with detailed notes.
Loud, animated, expressive.
Dominant, direct, opinionated communicator.
Quiet, reserved, listens more than speaks.
Monotone, structured, clarifying through questions.
Random visits, enjoys snacks and souvenirs.
Quick visit, targeted to favorite exhibits.
Stops at every exhibit, emotionally involved, charitable.
Planned route, systematic approach, avoids extra spending.
Enthusiastic, prolonged handshake or hug.
Firm, controlling grip.
Gentle, relaxed handshake, possibly using both hands.
Quick, firm, professional handshake.
Chronically late, uses tricks to try staying punctual.
Too busy, sacrifices personal time to accomplish tasks.
Attempts to be early, anxious about inconveniencing others.
Always punctual, meticulously manages time.
Multitasking with entertainment and social calls simultaneously.
Continues working, highly productive.
Enjoys quiet, relaxing activities.
Completes chores systematically.
Optimistic, sees good in everyone.
Competitive, aims to outperform others.
Compassionate, forgiving, gives multiple chances.
Critical, often sees incompetence.
Arrives late, occupies self with phone.
Demands punctuality, confronts delays assertively.
Patiently waits, understanding of delays.
On-time, frustrated if kept waiting.
Experimental, trendy hairstyles.
Stylish, professional, expensive.
Natural, low-maintenance styles.
Consistent, easy-care, rarely changes style.
Fun and enjoyment.
Challenge and financial success.
Serving and helping others.
Facts, data, and logical outcomes.
